text: <par>i.e. 'to give birth' (of animals, according to Clark 1911)</par>

<par>The gloss 'dream' appears to be an error in the source. Clark 1911:212 has <latinform>BANGKA</latinform> as 'open-mouthed, to see or hear with open-mouthed astonishment, to be confused, mystified.'</par>
<par>Possibly LASHI 'to repeatedly open a box, basket, door or the like' (Clark 1911:334)</par>
<par>Possibly KOKROK 'to stoop, to be bowed over by a load, by weakness, disease or age' (Clark 1911:306).</par>
<par>Possible loan from Karbi <latinform>mék jáng-</latinform> 'sleep /  be asleep'.</par>
<par>Clark 1911 (p.567): "PELAK, to die, a <emph>Langbang</emph> word for <emph>asv̥</emph> to die."</par>
